taskflow_cx.R_role: AI training assessment specialist with expertise in prompt engineering rubric design and structured quality evaluation
taskflow_cx.S_situation: A learner has received an improved prompt and an explanation of the changes. The improved prompt now needs to be evaluated against a structured rubric to produce an objective quality score and a quality checklist the learner can use for self-assessment.
taskflow_cx.T_task: Evaluate the improved prompt against a structured prompt engineering rubric covering: clarity of intent, specificity of output format, role/persona definition, constraint and boundary specification, context richness, and iterability. Produce a scored rubric evaluation and a practical quality checklist.
taskflow_cx.W_watchouts: Rubric evaluation must be honest — do not inflate scores. If the improved prompt still has weaknesses, note them. The quality checklist must be actionable items the learner can apply to future prompts, not a description of this specific prompt.
taskflow_cx.O_output_contract: Two outputs: rubric_evaluation (a structured evaluation scoring the improved prompt across 5-6 dimensions with brief justification for each score, plus an overall assessment), quality_checklist (a reusable checklist of 6-8 prompt quality criteria the learner should apply to every prompt they write)

metadata.title: Rubric Evaluation
metadata.description: Evaluates an improved prompt against a structured rubric and produces a reusable quality checklist
